methods.description: Process for extracting genomic DNA from soil samples, amplifying the fungal ITS1 region, and preparing samples for Illumina MiSeq sequencing.

steps.description: Genomic DNA was extracted from 0.25 g frozen soil using the PowerSoil DNA isolation kit (MoBio Laboratories, Inc.) according to the manufacturer's instructions.
steps.searchable_summary: extract genomic DNA from soil samples using PowerSoil DNA isolation kit

steps.description: DNA concentration of each sample was measured using a NanoDrop ND-1000 Spectrophotometer (NanoDrop Technologies).
steps.searchable_summary: measure DNA concentration using NanoDrop ND-1000 Spectrophotometer

steps.description: The fungal internal transcribed spacer 1 (ITS1) region was amplified using a two-step PCR procedure for Illumina MiSeq sequencing.
steps.searchable_summary: amplify ITS1 region using two-step PCR for sequencing

steps.description: The first amplification of the entire ITS region with primers ITS1F and ITS4 was carried out in a final 25 μl reaction solution.
steps.searchable_summary: perform first PCR amplification with ITS1F and ITS4 primers

steps.description: The product of the first amplification was diluted (×40) with sterilized deionized water and used as the template for the second PCR using the ITS5 and ITS2 primers linked to a barcode sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute first PCR product and perform second PCR with ITS5 and ITS2 primers

steps.description: PCR products were purified using a gel purification kit (Axygen), and 50 ng of DNA from each of the 240 samples was pooled and adjusted to 10 ng/µl.
steps.searchable_summary: purify PCR products and pool DNA samples

methods.description: Process for obtaining clean ITS1 sequences from raw data, including quality control, chimera detection, OTU clustering, and taxonomic assignment.

steps.description: Clean ITS1 sequences were obtained from raw sequences after quality control filtering using QIIME v. 1.7.0.
steps.searchable_summary: filter raw sequences for quality control using QIIME

steps.description: The ITS1 region of each remaining sequence was extracted using the fungal ITSX software package.
steps.searchable_summary: extract ITS1 region using ITSX software

steps.description: Potential chimeras were detected using the chimera.uchime command in Mothur by comparison with entries in the UNITE database.
steps.searchable_summary: detect chimeras using chimera.uchime in Mothur

steps.description: Nonchimeric ITS1 sequences were clustered into different operational taxonomic units (OTUs) at a 97% sequence similarity level using the UPARSE pipeline.
steps.searchable_summary: cluster sequences into OTUs using UPARSE

steps.description: A representative sequence of each OTU was selected and searched against the INSDC and UNITE databases using BLAST.
steps.searchable_summary: search representative OTU sequences against databases using BLAST

steps.description: Fungal OTUs were identified and assigned to functional groups following Tedersoo et al. (2014) and FunGuild.
steps.searchable_summary: assign OTUs to functional groups using Tedersoo et al. and FunGuild

steps.description: The number of sequences per sample was normalized to the smallest sample size using the sub.sample command in Mothur.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize sequence counts per sample using Mothur

methods.description: Description of study sites, sampling methods, and initial processing of soil samples for analysis.

steps.description: This study was conducted in 12 sites, including temperate, subtropical, and tropic forests in China.
steps.searchable_summary: select study sites in various forest types in China

steps.description: Longitude, latitude, and altitude at the sites were provided by the Chinese Forest Biodiversity Monitoring Network (CForBio).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain geographic data from CForBio

steps.description: Estimates of the mean annual temperature (MAT) and mean annual precipitation (MAP) were obtained from the WorldClim global climate data set.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch climate data from WorldClim

steps.description: In each site, one permanent forest plot was constructed by the CForBio.
steps.searchable_summary: construct permanent forest plots

steps.description: During June to October in 2014, 20 quadrats in each plot were randomly selected, and ten soil cores were randomly collected and mixed as one composite sample in each quadrat.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and composite soil samples from quadrats

steps.description: Soil samples were immediately packed in an ice box and transported to the laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: transport soil samples to laboratory

steps.description: Fresh soil samples were sieved through a 2-mm sieve to remove roots and debris.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve soil samples to remove debris

steps.description: Subsamples for DNA extraction were stored at −80°C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: store subsamples for DNA extraction at −80°C

methods.description: Calculation of climate change variables such as velocity and anomaly of MAT and MAP to study their effects on macroorganisms.

steps.description: The velocity and anomaly of MAT and MAP metrics have been widely applied to examine the effect of late Quaternary climate change on macroorganisms.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze effects of climate change using velocity and anomaly metrics

steps.description: We computed the velocity and anomaly for MAT and MAP between the last glacial maximum and the present.
steps.searchable_summary: compute velocity and anomaly for MAT and MAP

steps.description: To summarize LGM climate, we used estimates of past MAT and MAP from the Paleoclimate Modelling Intercomparison Project Phase II.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch LGM climate data from Paleoclimate Modelling Intercomparison Project
